is_p_falciparum.evidence: Study analyzes Plasmodium falciparum DHFR and DHPS genes from patient parasite samples.

reports_target_markers.evidence: Reports Pfdhfr codons 51, 59, 108 and Pfdhps codons 436, 437 (also examined 540, 581, 613).

reports_extractable_frequencies.evidence: Table 1 gives counts/percentages for DHFR 51/59/108; Table 2 gives DHPS 436/437 counts; text: '65% Ala, 24% Ser436, 26% Gly437'.

sub_country_location.evidence: All samples from a single drug trial at Albert Schweitzer Hospital, Lambaréné, Gabon; frequencies are pooled across the whole study population with no sub-site breakdown within one location. Only a single site is given (one town), which is sub-national, but frequencies are reported only as overall study totals.

temporal_resolution_within_3y.evidence: No explicit collection dates given; recruitment described in reference 11 (Lell et al. 1998). Paper does not state a collection year or window, so temporal placement cannot be established from this document.

african_field_samples.evidence: Human clinical isolates from children in a drug trial in Lambaréné, Gabon (African field samples).

is_primary_study.evidence: Primary empirical study analyzing patient parasite samples for point mutations.

duplicate_risk.evidence: Samples are from the same trial described in reference 11 (Lell et al. 1998, Am. J. Trop. Med. Hyg.): 'The recruitment of the patients is described in detail in reference 11.' Overlaps with a previously published cohort/trial.

supplement.evidence: No supplementary materials referenced; collection dates and any finer breakdown would come from the referenced trial paper (ref 11), not a supplement of this article.

assessment.summary: Drug trial in children in Lambaréné, Gabon assessing selection of SP-resistant P. falciparum. Reports extractable frequencies for Pfdhfr codons 51/59/108 and Pfdhps codons 436/437 (plus 540/581/613 examined) as pooled study totals. Single site is sub-national but frequencies are only overall totals; no explicit collection dates are given in this paper. Samples overlap with the previously published trial (ref 11), indicating high duplicate risk.
exclusion_reasons: No usable collection year/window reported in this paper; recruitment dates are in a separate referenced trial
Frequencies reported only as pooled study-wide totals from a single site without further breakdown; temporal binding cannot be established
High duplicate risk: samples derive from the trial already published as reference 11
